name: LambdaCDM cosmology + General Relativity + Standard Model
summary: The concordance baseline. General Relativity for gravity, the Standard Model for matter and gauge interactions, cold dark matter as a non-interacting non-relativistic fluid, and a cosmological constant. By construction this framework passes any benchmark whose 'requirement' is to reproduce GR / the SM / LambdaCDM in the appropriate regime; the open tensions in data/ are precisely the places where it does not pass cleanly. Predictions for all benchmarks are intentionally enumerated even where the answer is 'by_construction', so this file is a working template for new framework entries.

lineage.citation: Einstein, 'Die Feldgleichungen der Gravitation', Sitzungsberichte der Preussischen Akademie der Wissenschaften zu Berlin (1915)
Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics', Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)
Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', A&A 641, A6 (2020)

alpha_variation_atomic_clocks.note: SM has no mechanism for time-varying gauge couplings; alpha is a fixed Lagrangian parameter. alpha_dot / alpha = 0 by construction.
alpha_variation_atomic_clocks.reference: Standard Model Lagrangian; renormalization-group running at fixed reference scale gives a fixed alpha at any cosmological epoch.

antihydrogen_gravity_alpha_g.note: CPT + the equivalence principle require antimatter to fall like matter. a_gbar / g = +1 exactly in GR + SM.
antihydrogen_gravity_alpha_g.reference: Will, 'The Confrontation between General Relativity and Experiment', Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (2014)

axion_haloscope_admx.note: SM contains no QCD axion or ALP; the strong CP problem is unaddressed but no axion-photon coupling is predicted. Trivially below any haloscope/helioscope bound.
axion_haloscope_admx.reference: Standard Model Lagrangian (no PQ symmetry); strong_cp_theta_qcd benchmark records the unaddressed theta_QCD problem

dark_photon_kinetic_mixing.note: SM gauge group is SU(3) x SU(2) x U(1)_Y with no additional U(1)'. epsilon = 0 by construction.

electron_edm_jila.note: SM electron EDM from CKM CP violation alone is parametrically suppressed by GIM cancellation; short-distance four-loop estimates give |d_e^SM| ~ 10^-44 e cm, while long-distance hadron-level contributions can elevate it to ~10^-35 to 10^-38 e cm. Either way the SM prediction is at least five orders below the JILA bound 4.1e-30 e cm.
electron_edm_jila.reference: Yamaguchi and Yamanaka, 'Large long-distance contributions to the electric dipole moments of charged leptons in the standard model', PRL 125, 241802 (2020), arXiv:2003.08195

electron_g_minus_2_precision.note: The 2023 measurement a_e = 1.15965218059(13) x 10^-3 agrees with the SM five-loop QED prediction to ~10^-10. The remaining ~2.4 sigma tension is in the alpha-determination (Cs vs Rb interferometry), not in QED itself, so the SM is not falsified.
electron_g_minus_2_precision.reference: Aoyama, Kinoshita, Nio, Atoms 7, 28 (2019); Fan et al., PRL 130, 071801 (2023)

equivalence_principle_microscope.note: GR is a metric theory with universal coupling to matter; the Eotvos parameter eta vanishes identically.
equivalence_principle_microscope.reference: Will, 'The Confrontation between General Relativity and Experiment', Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (2014)

graviton_mass_gw_dispersion.note: GR is a massless spin-2 gauge theory; a graviton mass would break diffeomorphism invariance and is excluded by construction at the Lagrangian level.

graviton_speed_gw170817.note: GR predicts the tensor-mode equation h_ij;mu;mu = 0 on a Ricci-flat background, giving c_gw = c exactly at all orders in vacuum. Cosmological backgrounds yield O(H^2/k^2) corrections at the LIGO band that are unobservably small.
graviton_speed_gw170817.reference: Misner, Thorne, Wheeler, 'Gravitation' (1973), chapter 35; Maggiore, 'Gravitational Waves vol. 1' (2008)

inverse_square_law_short_distance.note: GR + SM predicts purely Newtonian gravity at sub-mm distances; no Yukawa correction. Equivalent to alpha = 0 at all lambda.
inverse_square_law_short_distance.reference: Standard treatment of weak-field GR; Will, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (2014)

katrin_neutrino_mass.note: The SM with right-handed neutrinos accommodates any neutrino mass spectrum below the cosmological sum bound (Sum m_nu < 0.072 eV from DESI+CMB+SN); KATRIN's effective electron-neutrino mass m_nu_e < 0.45 eV is well below this. The SM does not predict the masses, but is consistent with the measurement.
katrin_neutrino_mass.reference: Particle Data Group, 'Neutrino Properties' review, Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)

mu_e_conversion_sindrum.note: Charged-lepton flavor violation in the SM with massive neutrinos arises only via tiny GIM-suppressed neutrino loops; predicted R(mu Au -> e Au) ~ 10^-54, more than forty orders below the SINDRUM-II bound 7e-13. Effectively unobservable in the SM.
mu_e_conversion_sindrum.reference: Petcov, Sov. J. Nucl. Phys. 25, 340 (1977); Calibbi and Signorelli, Riv. Nuovo Cim. 41, 71 (2018), arXiv:1709.00294

muon_to_electron_gamma_meg.note: SM Br(mu+ -> e+ gamma) ~ (3 alpha / 32 pi) (Delta m^2_atm / m_W^2)^2 ~ 10^-54 from neutrino-mediated penguin diagrams, more than forty orders below the MEG bound 4.2e-13. Any observable signal is unambiguously BSM.
muon_to_electron_gamma_meg.reference: Petcov, Sov. J. Nucl. Phys. 25, 340 (1977); Calibbi and Signorelli, Riv. Nuovo Cim. 41, 71 (2018), arXiv:1709.00294

neutron_electric_dipole_moment.note: SM contributions to d_n have two pieces: (a) the QCD theta-term, with d_n_theta ~ e * theta_QCD * m_q / Lambda_QCD ~ 10^-16 e cm * theta_QCD - this saturates the bound at theta ~ 10^-10, the strong CP problem itself; (b) CKM CP violation, predicting d_n^SM(CKM) ~ 10^-32 e cm, well below the bound. Whether the SM 'passes' depends on whether one treats theta_QCD as a free parameter (open) or assumes a Peccei-Quinn or analogous mechanism (by_construction).
neutron_electric_dipole_moment.reference: Pospelov and Ritz, Annals Phys. 318, 119 (2005), arXiv:hep-ph/0504231; see also strong_cp_theta_qcd benchmark.

primordial_gw_tensor_to_scalar_ratio.note: Plain LambdaCDM does not specify the inflaton sector; r is determined by the inflationary potential. Most plateau-style large-field models (Starobinsky, Higgs inflation, alpha-attractors) predict r in the 10^-3 - 10^-2 range, comfortably below the BICEP/Keck bound 0.036; small-field and non-canonical models predict smaller r. Reproducing the bound is automatic for any inflaton model not already excluded.
primordial_gw_tensor_to_scalar_ratio.reference: Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. X. Constraints on inflation', A&A 641, A10 (2020), arXiv:1807.06211

proton_decay_lifetime.note: The SM has unbroken accidental B and L (and B-L) symmetries at the renormalizable level; baryon number is violated only by non-perturbative sphalerons that conserve B-L and are exponentially suppressed at low temperatures. The proton is stable in the SM.
proton_decay_lifetime.reference: 't Hooft, PRL 37, 8 (1976); Particle Data Group, 'Baryon-Number Violating Processes' review, Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)

sidm_self_interaction_clusters.note: Cold dark matter in LambdaCDM is by definition collisionless: sigma/m = 0 at the level of the model. The Harvey et al. cluster-merger bound sigma/m < 0.47 cm^2/g is satisfied trivially.
sidm_self_interaction_clusters.reference: Peebles and Ratra, RMP 75, 559 (2003); Harvey et al., Science 347, 1462 (2015), arXiv:1503.07675

strong_cp_theta_qcd.note: The SM accommodates any value of theta_QCD; the experimental bound |theta_QCD| < 1e-10 is the strong CP problem itself. Plain SM has no dynamical mechanism to suppress theta. Frameworks claiming to address this benchmark must invoke Peccei-Quinn (axion), a massless quark, or an analogous mechanism.
strong_cp_theta_qcd.reference: Peccei and Quinn, PRL 38, 1440 (1977); see strong_cp_theta_qcd benchmark for the bound.

sum_neutrino_masses_cosmology.note: The SM accommodates any neutrino mass spectrum consistent with oscillation data and the cosmological bound. Reproducing Sum m_nu < 0.072 eV is automatic provided the framework's neutrino mass spectrum is taken to satisfy the data.
sum_neutrino_masses_cosmology.reference: Particle Data Group, 'Neutrino Properties' review, Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)
